---
title: "nitrix.numerics.spatial"
generated: true
signature-hash: 5713cba3a7d23de7
description: "Volumetric shape and windowing utilities."
listing:
  id: nitrix-numerics-spatial-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-numerics-spatial/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.numerics.spatial`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Volumetric shape and windowing utilities.

The mundane-but-ubiquitous spatial pre- and post-processing that every
convolutional volume pipeline needs:

- [`pad_to_multiple`](nitrix-numerics-spatial/pad_to_multiple.qmd) and [`crop_to_multiple`](nitrix-numerics-spatial/crop_to_multiple.qmd) -- size each
  spatial axis up (or down) to a multiple of the network's pooling
  factor, returning the per-axis widths so the crop-back (and any
  affine-origin update, which is the container layer's job) is
  recoverable.
- [`nonzero_bounding_box`](nitrix-numerics-spatial/nonzero_bounding_box.qmd) -- the bounding box of the above-threshold
  region, as `(lo, hi)` index arrays.  Returning indices (not a crop)
  keeps the operation JIT-clean: the crop *shape* is data-dependent, the
  index arithmetic is not.
- [`gaussian_window`](nitrix-numerics-spatial/gaussian_window.qmd) and [`overlap_add`](nitrix-numerics-spatial/overlap_add.qmd) -- the two numeric
  pieces of sliding-window patch inference: a separable Gaussian patch
  weight and the weighted overlap-add normalisation
  $\sum(w \cdot \mathrm{patch}) / \sum w$.  The tiling and
  scheduling around them belong to the orchestration layer.

The spatial axes are the leading `spatial_rank` axes (channels-last
`(*spatial, c)` convention).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `*shape` | a variadic group of axes (`shape`) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-numerics-spatial-symbols}
:::
